title: mertensia ciliata var. stomatechoides
intro: Mertensia ciliata var. stomatechoides, also known as Bluebells or Lungwort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Traditionally, Mertensia ciliata var. stomatechoides is prepared as an infusion or decoction, and its herbal preparations can be consumed as a tea or used topically as a poultice to aid in the healing process.
